sentences.text: I. Medical Necessity Aetna considers magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) and computed tomography (CT) of the spine medically necessary when any of the following criteria is met: Clinical evidence of spinal stenosis; or Clinical suspicion of a spinal cord or cauda equina compression syndrome; or Congenital anomalies or deformities of the spine; or Diagnosis and evaluation of lumbar epidural lipomatosis; or Evaluation of recurrent symptoms after spinal surgery; or Evaluation prior to epidural injection to rule out tumor or infection and to delineate the optimal anatomical location for performing the injection; or Follow-up of evaluation for spinal malignancy or spinal infection; or Known or suspected myelopathy (e.g., multiple sclerosis) for initial diagnosis when MRI of the brain is negative or symptoms mimic those of other spinal or brainstem lesions; or Known or suspected primary spinal cord tumors (malignant or non-malignant); or Persistent back or neck pain with radiculopathy as evidenced by pain plus objective findings of motor or reflex changes in the specific nerve root distribution, and no improvement after 6 weeks of conservative therapyFootnote1*; or Primary spinal bone tumors or suspected vertebral, paraspinal, or intraspinal metastases; or Progressively severe symptoms despite conservative management; or Rapidly progressing neurological deficit, or major motor weakness; or Severe back pain (e.g., requiring hospitalization); or Spondylolisthesis and degenerative disease of the spine that has not responded to 4 weeks of conservative therapyFootnote1*; or Suspected infectious process (e.g., osteomyelitis epidural abscess of the spine or soft tissue); or Suspected spinal cord injury secondary to trauma; or Suspected spinal fracture and/or dislocation secondary to trauma (if plain films are not conclusive); or Suspected transverse myelitis.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Experimental, Investigational, or Unproven Aetna considers BoneMRI (MRI-based synthetic CT) experimental, investigational, or unproven for pre-operative assessments and surgical planning of the spine and the pelvis; and for diagnosing or monitoring of tumors (primary or metastatic).
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Aetna considers MRI and CT of the spine experimental, investigational, or unproven for all other indications because their clinical value for indications other than the ones listed above has not been established.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Clinical guidelines, including those from the Agency for Healthcare Policy and Research, have consistently recommended against routine imaging studies for acute low back pain.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Aetna considers the use of MRI for further evaluation of unstable injury in neurologically intact individuals with blunt trauma after a negative cervical spine CT result not medically necessary.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Aetna considers dynamic-kinetic MRI experimental, investigational, or unproven for evaluation of the cervical spine because its effectiveness has not been established.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Aetna considers the use of routine MRI after a normal CT of the cervical spine in obtunded or comatose individuals experimental, investigational, or unproven because the clinical value of this approach has not been established.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Aetna considers dual-energy CT experimental, investigational, or unrpoven for the evaluation of bone marrow edema and fracture lines in acute vertebral fractures because the clinical value of this approach has not been established. III.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: The authors concluded that lumbar imaging forLBP without indications of serious underlying conditions does not improve clinical outcomes and that clinicians should refrain from routine, immediate lumbar imaging in patients with acute or subacute LBP and without features suggesting a serious underlying condition.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that reliance on CT imaging alone to "clear the cervical spine" after blunt trauma can lead to missed injuries.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The findings of this study supported the addition of MRI in evaluating patients who are obtunded, or unexaminable, despite a negative CT scan.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: MRI / CT for Evaluation of Cervical Spinal Injury / Discontinuation of Cervical Collar Use Veiga and Mitchell (2019) noted that a missed cervical spinal injury could have devastating consequences.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: These researchers determined if MRI offered a definitive benefit over CT with respect to patient management.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that the diagnostic culture yield for CT-guided biopsies in cases of suspected spinal infection was low, approximately 33 %.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Advances in technology and hospital policy regarding specimen acquisition and tissue transfer and handling are needed to ensure the benefits of CT-guided biopsies out-weigh the risks.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Collaboration between interventional radiologists and pathologists is essential to optimize these techniques to ensure optimal results.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that there appeared to be a substantial subset of patients who developed morphological micro-instability after sole decompression procedures but did not experience any clinically significant effect of the instability.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that the prevalence of RNRs was body position-dependent; and increases in DCSA play a determinant role in resolving RNRs.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that changes in multifidus/erector spinae muscle CSA likely represented muscles stretching between upright and seated/flexed postures .
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that this research quantified the differences in spine structure measures that occurred in various experimental postures.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that the number of involved vertebral levels and obesity were strongly correlated, whereas severity of dural compression was not always significantly associated with neurological complications.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: In addition, weight-reduction therapy appeared to decrease the number of vertebral levels involved; and MRI- based grading is helpful for the diagnosis and evaluation of idiopathic lumbar SEL.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Gerardy and colleagues (2021) noted that SEL is a rare condition characterized by excessive accumulation of normal fat in the epidural space.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that DE-CT appeared to be a promising diagnostic tool for the detection of BME and disc edema, which can potentially replace the current gold standard, the MRI. Level of Evidence = II.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that the findings of this study demonstrated that both DE-CT and MRI provided high diagnostic confidence and image quality for the evaluation of acute vertebral fractures in general.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that CT-HU exhibited stronger correlations with DXA than MRI measurements.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that both vBMD detected by QCT and aBMD detected by DXA could discriminate fracture status in the spine, and vBMD performed a stronger correlation with fracture risk.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that all 4 methods, namely HU values, VBQ scores, QCT- measured BMD, and DXA-measured BMD can effectively distinguish between patients with and without vertebral fragile fractures.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that sCT could significantly enhance the diagnostic accuracy for patients suspected of having sacroiliitis, making it a valuable tool in clinical practice.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that these findings supported the use of sCT as a viable option for surgical planning, offering the advantage of reduced radiation exposure while maintaining diagnostic accuracy.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that sCT could be a valuable tool for surgical planning and intra- operative navigation in lumbar spine procedures.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that these findings suggested that sCT could effectively examine hip morphology, providing a radiation-free alternative for clinical evaluations.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that these findings showed that sCT could visualize bony structures in the cervical spine, suggesting its potential as a radiation-free alternative to conventional CT.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The results revealed strong agreement between sCT and CT measurements, indicating that sCT could serve as a reliable alternative for assessing hip morphology without the associated radiation exposure of CT.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that these findings suggested that sCT may be beneficial for pre-operative assessments and surgical planning.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that these results suggested that sCT could be a valuable tool for monitoring musculoskeletal abnormalities in pediatric patients, especially in cases where traditional CT is limited due to radiation exposure concerns.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that these findings underscored the importance of sCT in enhancing diagnostic accuracy and identifying clinically significant incidental findings.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that the results indicated high precision and reliability in measurements, suggesting that this innovative protocol could enhance the study of scoliosis and improve surgical navigation.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that these findings suggested that sCT is a reliable alternative to cCT for evaluating hip morphology in children, offering the advantage of reduced radiation exposure.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that these findings suggested that sCT could be a valuable tool for monitoring structural spine damage in axSpA patients, especially in clinical trials.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that both 1-mm semi-coronal T1w-gradient echo (GRE) and 1-mm semi-coronal sCT increased sensitivity and reader confidence for the detection of erosion, sclerosis and ankylosis, compared with standard 4 mm T1w-turbo spin-echo (TSE).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that this case highlighted the potential of MRI-generated sCT in pediatric spondylolysis management, providing a comprehensive assessment of bony and soft tissue pathology without the ionizing radiation associated with traditional CT.
sentences.label: PREMISE
